text: We introduce redMaPPer, a new red-sequence cluster finder designed to make efficient use of current and future survey data. This version of the algorithm has a number of highly desirable features: - Self-training to (optionally) create a robust red-sequence model given a small training set, an issue for large surveys at high redshift) - Handling of complex masks with varying depth - Capability to create large numbers of random points useful for large scale structure studies - Full posterior distribution of redshift for each cluster found - Multiple galaxies are identified as potential centers for each cluster with associated centering posterior distributions - Highly parallel and efficient, (running a DES-like catalog down to 24 takes approximately 500 CPU hours) - Good photoz, tight correlation between measured richness and true mass, and extremely high completeness and purity. We utilize 10,000 deg^2 of SDSS DR8, producing a sample of 25k high purity clusters with redshifts between 0.08-0.55. The photo-z distributions of the redMaPPer clusters are nearly Gaussian, with an intrinsic scatter of \sigma_z\approx0.006 at z=0.1 increasing out to \sigma_z\approx0.02 at z=0.5. The median value for |\\Delta z|/(1+z) for the full sample is 0.006. Also, the impact from projected clusters is shown to be less than 5%. For a detailed comparison with X-ray and SZ cluster samples using redMaPPer DR8, see Rozo & Rykoff (2014).
